key_insight: Sliding-window attention becomes much more viable for math reasoning when architecture-aware reinforcement learning is added after supervised conversion from self-attention.
engineering_relevance: The paper suggests a practical path to long-context efficiency: replace quadratic self-attention with linear-cost sliding-window attention without training a new base model from scratch.
method: Convert a pretrained self-attention model to sliding-window attention with supervised fine-tuning.
Apply on-policy reinforcement learning so the model adapts trajectories under the sliding-window constraint.
Use architecture-aware training to reduce the mismatch between long-range SFT data and local attention.
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning benchmarks, the two-stage SWARR recipe recovers much of the accuracy lost during sliding-window conversion while keeping efficiency benefits.
watch_item: Results may depend on how well the RL data and reward setup align with the sliding-window inductive bias; gains could be smaller if long-range dependencies remain essential.
paper_title: Architecture-Aware Reinforcement Learning Makes Sliding-Window Attention Competitive in Math Reasoning
